video:video.video:title: 21st century tech brings Sistine Chapel frescoes to London
video:video.video:description: The Sistine Chapel has come to London - in digital form. A stunning new installation is opening which brings to life Michelangelo’s masterpiece with the help of projections and animated characters.

video:video.video:title: Martha Lillard, last US polio patient using iron lung, dies at 78 in Oklahoma
video:video.video:description: Martha Lillard had just turned 5 when she was diagnosed with polio and depended on an iron lung to live.

video:video.video:title: Esports World Cup kicks off in Paris amid accusations of “sportswashing”
video:video.video:description: As thousands of players throng to Paris to participate, the Saudi-backed tournament faces accusations that it's part of the kingdom’s strategy to "wash" its image.

video:video.video:title: Billions for DHS, $20 million for body cameras yet officers in Houston shooting didn't have them
video:video.video:description: Democratic congresswoman Sylvia Garcia said that ICE Director David Venturella promised her that the agency will have body cameras on all officers in the field by the end of the month.

video:video.video:title: The Bayeux Tapestry is back on English soil after almost 1,000 years
video:video.video:description: After almost 1,000 years, the Bayeux Tapestry is back on English soil. In scenes like a heist movie in reverse, the priceless medieval artwork was spirited into the British Museum on Friday in the dead of night, after a high-tech, tight-security operation where any slip-up could have spelled disast

video:video.video:title: 8 men indicted in planned drone and sniper attack on White House UFC cage-fighting show
video:video.video:description: Eight men were indicted on murder and terrorism conspiracy charges Thursday for their alleged roles in a thwarted drone and sniper attack on the UFC cage-fighting show staged at the White House in June.

video:video.video:title: Trump ousts bipartisan election commissioners in latest push to reshape US voting
video:video.video:description: President Donald Trump has ousted members of a bipartisan federal election commission charged with assisting state and local election officials by distributing grants, testing voting systems, and maintaining national voter registration forms.
video:video.video:tag: Supreme Court of the United States

video:video.video:title: Bears in a Kosovo sanctuary receive cold treats to cope with heat
video:video.video:description: As temperatures soared across the Balkans and Europe, staff at a bear sanctuary near Kosovo's capital, Pristina, were preparing frozen treats to help the animals cope with the heat.

video:video.video:title: Colombia’s court on the conflict with FARC rebels in limbo as president-elect vows to dismantle it
video:video.video:description: A decade after the Colombian government signed a historic peace accord with a now-defunct rebel group, the tribunal handling crimes related to the conflict faces an uncertain future after the country's president-elect vowed to dismantle it.

video:video.video:title: What to know about cyclospora, the parasite linked to severe diarrhea
video:video.video:description: Michigan is experiencing its largest outbreak of a parasitic infection that causes severe diarrhea. Cyclospora spreads through contaminated food and water, often linked to fresh produce.

video:video.video:title: ICE arrests 10,000 in 5 days
video:video.video:description: Immigration and Customs Enforcement arrested 10,000 people over a five-day period at the end of June, marking a major push by the agency tasked with carrying out the Trump administration’s mass deportations agenda.

video:video.video:title: Mexican man killed in Houston ICE shooting was not the target of operation, lawmaker says
video:video.video:description: A U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement officer fatally shot Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, a Mexican national who lived in the U.S. for decades, as the homebuilder drove his construction crew to a Houston job site.

video:video.video:title: Emergency services say number of injured in Russian airstrike in Zaporizhzhia has risen to 29
video:video.video:description: The number of people injured in a Russian airstrike on Zaporizhzhia rose to 29, including two children, Ukraine's State Emergency Service said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Suki Waterhouse talks new album; love for her daughter
video:video.video:description: Suki Waterhouse is loving life these days with partner Robert Pattinson and their daughter, while releasing her new album, "Loveland." (July 10)

video:video.video:title: UK police arrest a suspect in the killing of former Parliament member Ann Widdecombe
video:video.video:description: British police on Friday arrested a 26-year-old man on suspicion of murder in the killing of Ann Widdecombe, a former British member of Parliament and reality TV contestant.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 10
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Friday, July 10th: Trump ousts members of bipartisan election commission; testimony concludes in Charlie Kirk assassination case; scrutiny grows over fatal ICE shooting in Houston; Spain wildfire kills more than a dozen people.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter describes courtroom scene in Charlie Kirk killing case
video:video.video:description: A preliminary hearing concluded Friday for the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

video:video.video:title: 'The Odyssey' stars talk about the 'overwhelming' adventure from Chris Nolan
video:video.video:description: Matt Damon, Anne Hathaway, Tom Holland and Zendaya discuss working with Christopher Nolan for his upcoming blockbuster film, "The Odyssey." (July 10)

video:video.video:title: Next steps after Graham Platner formally withdraws from Maine Senate race
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has submitted paperwork to formally withdraw from the Maine Senate race, kicking off Democrats’ quest for a nominee they hope can defeat Republican Sen.

video:video.video:title: Emergency crews rescue about 20 after a campground building collapses in Missouri flooding
video:video.video:description: Emergency crews rescued about 20 people in Missouri on Friday after a building collapsed in a campground following widespread flooding and heavy rainfall in the state.

video:video.video:title: Many English-speaking fans are turning to Spanish broadcasts for the World Cup
video:video.video:description: Thanks to the excitement of Spanish commentary, the iconic 'goooooooal' call, and the lack of commercial breaks, Spanish-language broadcasts have become a favorite among many World Cup viewers in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Next steps after Graham Platner formally withdraws from Maine Senate race
video:video.video:description: Graham Platner has submitted paperwork to formally withdraw from the Maine Senate race, kicking off Democrats’ quest for a nominee they hope can defeat Republican Sen.

video:video.video:title: Seafarers attacked in the Strait of Hormuz sue shipping company in Thailand
video:video.video:description: Three former crew members of a Thai cargo ship that was struck in the Strait of Hormuz in March filed a lawsuit Friday against the vessel’s operator over labor rights violations and unfair dismissal.

video:video.video:title: Fourth running of the bulls in Pamplona leaves several runners injured
video:video.video:description: The fourth San Fermin bull run was once again a fast and dangerous dash along the cobbled streets of Pamplona.

video:video.video:title: British nationals who fled their homes in Spain due to wildfires describe their experience
video:video.video:description: British expats living in the area of Spain which was devastated by wildfires have spoken of panic as residents were told to evacuate.

video:video.video:title: Meteorologists warn of week of dangerous US temperatures: 'Heat is not to be played with'
video:video.video:description: Most of America's Lower 48 states are about to swelter under an unusually large, strong and long-lasting heat dome that will spike temperatures.

video:video.video:title: Thai zoo celebrates famous pygmy hippo's second birthday
video:video.video:description: A zoo in Thailand celebrated the 2nd birthday of the viral sensation pygmy hippo Moo Deng on Friday, with a large cake made of fruits and vegetables.

video:video.video:title: Firefighters battle wildfire in Almeria that claimed at least 11 lives, 19 others are missing
video:video.video:description: A wildfire in southern Spain has killed at least 11 people, making it one of the country's deadliest on record, as soaring temperatures grip much of the country, authorities said Friday.

video:video.video:title: Woman says her entire family is trapped under rubble after building collapse in Venezuela
video:video.video:description: Stefany Landaez had been living in Trinidad and Tobago for nine years with her husband and two children. But they made the decision to come back to Venezuela.

video:video.video:title: Electric tricycles carry Cubans amid fuel shortage
video:video.video:description: Although electric tricycles are common in many countries, in Cuba they are now the primary mode of transportation due to a severe fuel shortage caused by a U.S blockade.

video:video.video:title: Zendaya was informed of her 'Odyssey' role by Tom Holland
video:video.video:description: "The Odyssey" star Zendaya say she went from cheering on Tom Holland and his role in the film to learning from him that she was also offered the role of Athena.

video:video.video:title: Egypt soccer fans celebrate team's World Cup achievement as they return home
video:video.video:description: Thousands of fans turned out Friday to welcome home Egypt's national soccer team after its World Cup run ended with a 3-2 loss to defending champion Argentina in the round of 16.

video:video.video:title: Much of America is about to swelter under climate-fueled heat dome
video:video.video:description: Most of America’s Lower 48 states are about to swelter under an unusually large, strong and long-lasting heat dome.

video:video.video:title: Dua Lipa opens a library in Portugal
video:video.video:description: Dua Lipa opens a library in Portugal

video:video.video:title: AP Top Stories July 10
video:video.video:description: Here’s the latest for Friday, July 10th: DHS says ICE agents weren’t wearing body cameras during Houston shooting; Lawyers for Charlie Kirk shooting suspect’s court hearing focused on evidence; Deadly wildfire in Spain; Maine Democrats to hold convention to pick Senate candidate.

video:video.video:title: Inside Israel's push to clear sea munitions as part of global push to protect waters
video:video.video:description: Israel's has launched a project aimed at clearing part of the seas to give beach space back to the population.

video:video.video:title: Indian Prime Minister Modi welcomed at Government House in New Zealand
video:video.video:description: Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi was welcomed at Government House in Auckland on Saturday with a traditional ceremony and a troop review.

video:video.video:title: Spain fanatics sing and march to Los Angeles Stadium ahead of World Cup match against Belgium
video:video.video:description: A crowd of Spanish fans sang and celebrated outside the Los Angeles Stadium in Inglewood, California before marching to cheer their team on as Spain takes on Belgium for the World Cup quarterfinals.

video:video.video:title: Detroit-based startup believes AI-powered drone technology could ease truck parking shortage problem
video:video.video:description: A Detroit-based drone company is monitoring trucking sites using AI-powered drone technology. It’s part of an effort to address the challenge of truck parking shortages.

video:video.video:title: IAEA chief Grossi meets Rosatom head in Russia's Kaliningrad
video:video.video:description: International Atomic Energy Agency Director General Rafael Grossi met Rosatom chief Alexei Likhachev in Russia's exclave of Kaliningrad on Friday for talks focused on the situation at the Russian-controlled Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant in Ukraine.

video:video.video:title: Nigeria’s eroding Orimedu coast threatens homes and livelihoods
video:video.video:description: Communities along the coastline of Nigeria are struggling to cope with heavy rainfall and unstable, sandy soils. Residents of the Orimedu community are living in fear.

video:video.video:title: World Cup fans share the best food they've tried in America
video:video.video:description: "In-N-Out and Fatburger are our favorite:" World Cup fans reveal the best food they've tried since being in the U.S.

video:video.video:title: Thousands arrive at the Srebrenica Memorial Center ahead of commemorations
video:video.video:description: Thousands marched to the Bosnian village of Potocari on Friday to commemorate the victims of the Srebrenica genocide.

video:video.video:title: Come with me to cover a World Cup match
video:video.video:description: Spain reached the World Cup semifinals with a 2-1 win over Belgium, and AP reporter Sarah Jones-Smith was there. Here's a look at the action.

video:video.video:title: Fans go full Pitbull in London for World Record attempt
video:video.video:description: Pitbull fans have broken a world record by dressing up as the music superstar. Ahead of Mr Worldwide's performance at BST Hyde Park on Friday, Guinness World Record officials counted the largest gathering of people wearing bald caps.

video:video.video:title: AP reporter witnesses phone call between Pope Leo XIV and his brother
video:video.video:description: AP reporter witnesses phone call between Pope Leo XIV and his brother

video:video.video:title: How some fermented foods benefit our gut health
video:video.video:description: Research shows fermented foods are healthy for your gut but there aren't clear recommendations on how often to eat them or which are the best.

video:video.video:title: New Zealand prime minister on potential of New Zealand-India relationship during Modi visit
video:video.video:description: New Zealand Prime Minister Christopher Luxon met with his Indian counterpart during the latter's trip to Auckland on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: "We're already living in a high-risk fire climate in Europe," warns researcher amid deadly southern Spanish blaze
video:video.video:description: One of Spain's deadliest wildfires on record killed 12 people overnight into Friday as soaring temperatures grip much of the country.

video:video.video:title: 'Five minutes later, a second one followed': People in Kyiv recount overnight Russian strikes
video:video.video:description: At least 11 people, including a child, were wounded after Russia launched an overnight missile and drone attack on Kyiv early Saturday, according to Ukraine’s State Emergency Service.

video:video.video:title: Multiple runners injured on chaotic fifth day of Spain's San Fermin bull run festival in Pamplona
video:video.video:description: One runner was gored in the face and many more were fortunate to not be seriously injured during a chaotic bull run at Spain’s San Fermin festival on Saturday.

video:video.video:title: Bodies of newly identified Srebrenica victims buried on day commemorating 1995 genocide
video:video.video:description: The remains of ten victims of the 1995 Srebrenica massacre, Europe’s only acknowledged genocide since World War II, were buried on Saturday at a memorial center.

video:video.video:title: Why is the internet obsessed with Erling Haaland?
video:video.video:description: Erling Haaland is one of soccer’s biggest stars, but it’s his goofy personality, viral moments and off-the-pitch charm that have made him an internet favorite.

video:video.video:title: Woman freed after 11-hour hostage situation in Berlin supermarket, suspect detained
video:video.video:description: A woman was freed after being held hostage at a supermarket in southern Berlin in an hours-long standoff late Friday, officials said.

video:video.video:title: Islandwide blackout strikes Cuba for the second time this week as its grid crumbles
video:video.video:description: An islandwide blackout struck Cuba on Friday for the second time this week as the nation of nearly 10 million people grapples with a crumbling power grid and fuel shortages stemming from a U.S. energy blockade.

video:video.video:title: Hundreds of firefighters battle wildfire in southern Spain that killed at least 12
video:video.video:description: Hundreds of firefighters backed by helicopters and fixed-wing aircraft struggled Saturday to contain one of Spain’s deadliest wildfires that killed a least 12 people.

video:video.video:title: New Orleans re-creates Pamplona's San Fermin, Spain's running of the bulls
video:video.video:description: For the 20th year in a row New Orleans put on it's version of San Fermin festival in Pamplona, Spain's Running of the Bulls where participants dress in traditional white and red clothing while being chased by roller skaters acting as bulls.

video:video.video:title: Haaland look-alike contest takes over Miami after beauty influencer’s viral post
video:video.video:description: A content creator who went viral earlier this week for supposedly looking like Erlign Haaland hosted a look-alike contest dedicated to the Norway star, ahead of his team’s match against England in the FIFA World Cup.

video:video.video:title: Mexican builder fatally shot by an ICE officer is mourned after making a life in the US
video:video.video:description: Four Democratic members of Congress who represent the Houston area said at a vigil Saturday that they would push for an independent investigation into the shooting and killing of builder Lorenzo Salgado Araujo.

video:video.video:title: AP top stories July 11
video:video.video:description: Here's the latest for Saturday, July 11th: U.S. and Iranian leaders have trade threats, Russian bombs Ukraine's northern Sumy region, a vigil is held in Houston for Lorenzo Salgado Araujo, New Orleans holds its version of Spain's San Fermin bull run festival.

video:video.video:title: 10 killed after small plane crashes in the Bahamas, musicians among the victims
video:video.video:description: A small plane crashed in the Bahamas on Friday, killing 10 people and prompting the government to temporarily ground Flamingo Air flights.

video:video.video:title: See the stunning Buddhist temple aiming for UNESCO World Heritage status
video:video.video:description: A 13th century temple in Thailand could soon secure a place on the UNESCO World Heritage List. Wat Phra Mahathat Woramahawihan is considered a jewel of Buddhist worship.

video:video.video:title: 'We ain’t seen nothing yet,’ says ABBA's Björn Ulvaeus on AI-generated music
video:video.video:description: Björn Ulvaeus helped write some of the best-selling pop songs of the 20th century. Today, he's President of CISAC, the global body representing more than five million creators across music, film, literature and the arts, and one of the most active lobbyists on what AI owes the artists it's trained o

video:video.video:title: Eye and brainwave tech help artist with ALS find new stage as DJ
video:video.video:description: ALS, or amyotrophic lateral sclerosis, hasn’t stopped one artist in Tokyo from taking to the stage to perform with eye movements, AI and brain signals.

video:video.video:title: US attacks Iran over ship being hit in Strait of Hormuz; Tehran lashes out again at Gulf Arab states
video:video.video:description: The United States attacked Iran early Sunday morning. The U.S. military’s Central Command said it hit some 140 targets in the strikes, far more than the last two rounds.
